claim_boundary: Mesh confidence covers only registered parent/child boundaries and current consumed evidence; child-local green or background progress is not parent proof.
do_not_use_when: The work is an ordinary single model, a test hierarchy, or a code structure split.

downstream_targets.condition: Leaf/closure transitions need obligation, owner-code, and test binding.
Leaf/closure cells need child-suite ownership and freshness.
Whole-flow parent confidence consumes guard-family closure evidence.

native_checks.summary: Run the tracked hierarchical model-mesh and reattachment tests.

route_summary: Govern model hierarchy through target split derivation, disjoint ownership, current child reattachment, receipts, siblings, and closure liveness.

source_requirements.summary: Target children derive from a named source model and cover parent partitions without unsafe overlap.
Parent confidence consumes current child evidence, runtime paths, coverage receipts, and stable handoff contracts.
Missing child-output consumers, joins, feedback, progress/bounds, or affected-sibling review blocks broad parent closure.

use_when: Parent confidence depends on several/oversized models, changed child boundaries, stale child evidence, coverage receipts, affected siblings, or whole-flow closure.

workflow.summary: Name the parent claim, child set, model level, split/reattachment trigger, and required scope.
Inventory target split, partition ownership, child contracts, evidence tiers/freshness, receipts, siblings, and closure tokens.
Review coverage/disjointness, current reattachment, parent receipt consumption, affected siblings, leaf boundaries, and closure liveness.
Run the hierarchical mesh model and reject overlap, stale/unconsumed evidence, missing closure, or no-delta loops.
Return parent/child confidence only for current consumed evidence and route unresolved cells/receipts to owners.
